preview_text:
The model favors Mexico decisively, assigning a 71% win probability against a Czech Republic chance of just 7%. This represents a substantial gap in the forecast, with Mexico clearly positioned as the stronger team in the model's estimation.

The 23% draw probability suggests meaningful competitive balance in the underlying match dynamics, despite Mexico's clear edge. The win spread—a 64 percentage-point gap between the favorite and underdog—indicates low likelihood of a Czech Republic victory, though not an elimination of the possibility. The match shape leans toward a Mexico win or stalemate rather than an upset.

Model and sportsbook readings diverge sharply here. The books trim Mexico's win probability to 52%, a 18 percentage-point gap from the model, and assign Czech Republic 21% compared to the model's 7%. The books see a substantially more competitive contest than the statistical forecast does. Polymarket prices reflect near-complete indifference across all three outcomes. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is correct.
